# Project Hub

*Auto-generated by `render_hub.py` — do not edit manually (except State of Knowledge).*

**Phase**: Phase: TEST
**Active nodes**: I05 (), I06 (), I07 (), I09 (), I10 (), I11 (), I12 (), I13 (), E20 (), D-008 ()

## State of Knowledge

<!-- STATE-OF-KNOWLEDGE-START -->

### What we know
- **Smaller-model anchor (qwen3.6-27b) replicates the regime-dependence
  pattern (overnight 2026-05-02, 1 seed × 17 tasks).** Phase A (F0-eq,
  no augmentation): 0.882 mean (17/17 scored, 0 failures). Phase B
  (F4-eq, xmllint MCP + plugin v3): 0.902 on 16 common tasks (+0.008
  over A); 0.849 with failures-as-0 (1 timeout on
  ExampleIsothermalLeakyWell). Compare DSv4 F0 0.910 → F4 0.921
  (+0.011 over 3 seeds). Augmentation effect on common tasks is
  comparable in magnitude on both models. **Striking single-task lift**:
  TutorialPoroelasticity 0.346 → 0.689 (+0.343 on qwen) — augmentations
  help most on tasks where the base struggles. **Caveat**: 1 seed only;
  augmented stack introduced a 6% timeout rate (1/17) on qwen that did
  not appear on DSv4. See `docs/2026-05-02_autonomous-campaign-results.md`
  §"Phase 4 — qwen3.6-27b" and `docs/2026-05-02_efficiency-table.md`.
- **Paper-ready efficiency table available** (overnight 2026-05-02):
  per-cell tools, turns, wall-time, and tools-before-Write across
  9 Phase 2 cells (51 runs each) plus qwen Phase 4. Headline:
  **F0 baseline 82 tools / 73 tools-before-Write; SE 69 tools / 60
  tools-before-Write at +0.010 quality** — efficiency without quality
  sacrifice on the Self-Evolved plugin cell. F4 (xmllint+memory) ties
  SE on quality (0.921 vs 0.919) but needs ~80 tools, vs SE's 69.
  See `docs/2026-05-02_efficiency-table.md` for the table and
  `scripts/efficiency_table.py` for the generator.
- **Sub-agent orchestrator on DSv4-flash, 17/17 succeeded — but the
  +0.204 vs vanilla DSv4-flash is PRELIMINARY** (XN-018, n=1 seed).
  Adversarial code review (RN-005) flagged 3 P1 blockers BEFORE the
  number can be propagated: (1) cross-test-task GT leakage —
  `contamination.py:get_blocked_files_for_task` only blocks the current
  task's GT; ExampleIsothermalLeakyWell trace shows the orchestrator
  copied `thermalLeakyWell_base.xml` (GT for a *sibling* test task)
  into its working dir; fix is one line (use `union_xml` already in
  `test_blocklist.json`); (2) `--disallowedTools Write` not enforced
  (multi-flag instead of comma-joined) — `Write` fired on 4 tasks
  including TutorialSneddon (Δ +0.754); (3) token totals 2–4× inflated
  by JSONL `message.usage` double-counting under subagent fan-out.
  Three of the largest-win tasks (Sneddon, IsothermalLeakyWell,
  DPWellbore) are implicated. Re-run required under fixed blocklist
  + fixed Write-deny + multi-seed before any quantitative claim.
  Architecture itself works mechanically (17/17 ran end-to-end);
  attribution to "segmentation" is downgraded to a hypothesis pending
  primer-matched controls.
- **Plugin wins on deepseek-v3.2 at model parity.** Paired comparison of
  E03 (plugin + ds) vs E01 (no-plugin + ds) on 35 common-scored tasks:
  mean TreeSim **0.828 vs 0.653 (+0.178)**, pass >=0.7 **88.6% vs 54.3%**.
  Wins on 29/35 tasks, max loss -0.17. See XN-001.
- **Plugin wins also on minimax-m2.7 (cross-model).** E06 (no-plugin +
  minimax) vs E02 (plugin + minimax) on paired 15 test tasks: mean
  TreeSim **0.694 vs 0.809 (+0.102)**. Plugin wins 11/15. Smaller delta
  than deepseek, consistent with minimax being a stronger base model.
  See XN-005.
- **Plugin gain concentrates on catastrophic-failure rescue (deepseek).**
  Biggest wins are all tasks where no-plugin deepseek wrote essentially
  empty/wrong XML (TutorialSneddon 0.099 -> 0.804; ExampleDPWellbore
  0.305 -> 0.992; ExampleMandel 0.275 -> 0.948).
- **Mechanism for the plugin gains characterized (XN-008).** Direct
  reading of agent trajectories on the 3 biggest-delta tasks confirms
  **genuine signal, not variance/luck**. Plugin provides: (a) semantic
  discovery of ALTERNATE solver architectures the agent would
  otherwise miss (Sneddon: no-plugin finds only LagrangianContact;
  plugin discovers EmbeddedFractures, LagrangianContact, and
  HydroFracture via RAG queries on "embedded fracture surface
  generation"); (b) schema-aware filtering (DPWellbore: no-plugin
  reads 13 files including source .hpp/.cpp and confuses
  DruckerPrager variants; plugin's `search_navigator` on
  "SolidMechanicsLagrangianFEM Drucker Prager plasticity" filters
  to wellbore context directly); (c) variant disambiguation (Mandel:
  no-plugin can't distinguish FIM vs sequential benchmark variants;
  plugin's semantic doc retrieval returns variant-purpose context).
  Grep/Glob cannot match these because files don't share filename
  patterns and source-code keywords match irrelevant files.
- **On minimax the rescue pattern differs** — minimax-no-plugin already
  scores well on Sneddon (0.493, vs deepseek-no-plugin's 0.099); plugin
  LOSES on Sneddon in minimax. Plugin matters more for weaker base
  models, less for stronger ones. Expected.

### What's working
- Current `repo3` plugin as-is: 3-DB ChromaDB RAG + `geos-rag` skill.
- 20-min timeout on deepseek — bumped from 10 min after ablation_v1 showed
  36% of tasks ran >600s. Timeout accounting: E01 (no-plugin+ds) had **3
  timeouts** (IsothermalLeakyWell, TFrac, DeadOilEgg); E03 (plugin+ds)
  had **5 timeouts** (CasedContactThermoElastic, kgdExperimentValidation,
  pknViscosityDominated, DeadOilEgg, SPE11b). Plugin trades timeout
  burden: rescues IsothermalLeakyWell/TFrac, adds 4 others. Net +2
  timeouts under the plugin. Does NOT reduce timeouts overall. SPE11b
  was misreported earlier as "parse error" — it is a timeout that left
  partial XML that the scorer could not parse.
- XMLTreeSim metric discriminates cleanly; per-section sub-scores are
  useful for diagnosing which phases of the XML the agent gets wrong.

### What failed
- **qwen3.5-9b ablation: 0/36 success.** Model doesn't reliably follow CC
  tool-use format; exits after ~3 turns without writing files. Capability
  ceiling of the base model. Sub-10B models are out of scope.
- **Frozen-cheatsheet memory (E04, 1700 tokens) HURTS performance.**
  0.532 vs plugin-only 0.831 on paired 14 tasks (-0.322). Wins 1/14.
  Plugin had 17/17 successes; memory had 13/17 (3 failed_no_outputs
  + 1 timeout). Failure signature: agent emits `redacted_thinking`
  block then `end_turn` without calling Write/Edit. See XN-003.
- **Short cheatsheet (E05, ~300 tokens) ALSO fails** with the same
  signature. 13/17 success, mean 0.561 on 14 scored (vs plugin-only
  0.831). Same failed_no_outputs on ModifiedCamClay +
  ExtendedDruckerPrager; adds 2 new timeouts (EDPWellbore + Mandel
  both succeeded under E04). Confirms **length is not the cause.**
  Any cheatsheet-in-system-prompt triggers the failure on a subset
  of tasks. See XN-004. Both designs discarded; memory via
  system-prompt-inject is a dead end on this model.
- **CC native memory is a no-op in our pipeline.** `memory_paths`
  is set per-task but directory is empty across sampled tasks. Zero
  `memory`-named tool calls in 36 E03 tasks. `/memory` slash command
  absent from agent's command list. Per-task workspace is wiped with
  container anyway. Not a confound for any result.
- **Filetree injection (E07) ALSO hurts plugin.** Added a precomputed
  `/geos_lib/inputFiles/` path index (~1100 tokens, 746 files / 87
  dirs) to system prompt; paired mean TreeSim **0.604 vs plugin-only
  0.831 = delta -0.227** on 17 tasks. 0 redacted_thinking failures
  (structural content doesn't trigger cheatsheet-style stops), but
  XML quality degrades severely — regresses most on the tasks that
  plugin rescues most (Sneddon -0.719, DPWellbore -0.687, Mandel
  -0.637). Filetree seems to distract agent from semantic RAG path.
  See XN-007.
- **Memory failure mechanism characterized (XN-009).** All 6 augmentations
  fire UPFRONT and disrupt the plugin's iterative semantic-discovery RAG
  loop. Three distinct mechanisms: (1) cheatsheet anchoring — agent
  pattern-matches on filenames; (2) filetree path hijacking — skips RAG;
  (3) memory_lookup poisoning — wrong-physics priors returned with high
  confidence (Sneddon→hydrofracture, Mandel→hydrofracture, DPWellbore→
  triaxialDriver). Frozen memory built from 18 train tasks structurally
  cannot help on test tasks whose physics family isn't represented in
  train. Insight: **memory should constrain search space, not short-
  circuit reasoning**; tested via E12 (gated memory, threshold + delay).
- **Six convergent memory/augmentation negatives across channels.**

  | Attempt | Delta | Channel | Content |
  |---|---:|---|---|
  | E04 long cheatsheet | -0.322 | sys-prompt | abstract rules |
  | E05 short cheatsheet | -0.270 | sys-prompt | abstract rules |
  | E07 filetree | -0.227 | sys-prompt | structural paths |
  | E08 minimal primer | -0.235 | sys-prompt (shrink) | primer content |
  | E09 workspace cheatsheet | -0.212 | /workspace/file | abstract rules |
  | **E11 G-memory MCP tool** | **-0.192** | **MCP tool** | concrete examples |

  MCP-tool is least-bad (partial rescue on DPWellbore, +0.142 on pkn)
  but still negative. System-prompt-inject variants trigger the
  `redacted_thinking -> end_turn` failure; non-system-prompt variants
  just produce lower-quality XML + more timeouts. The rescue mechanism
  on TutorialSneddon + ExampleMandel is fragile — breaks under any
  perturbation regardless of channel/content.

### What's unresolved
- **Single seed per condition.** No cross-seed std on +0.175. Non-blocker
  for advisor meeting; required before paper.
- **Plugin attribution (I11).** Skill-prompt contribution vs MCP-RAG
  contribution is entangled. Need skill-only and MCP-only runs.
- **Stacking story.** D-001 memory design failed. E05 short-cheatsheet
  also failed. "Adaptations stack" cannot currently be claimed via
  system-prompt-inject memory. Remaining memory variants to test
  (MCP-tool-on-demand, stronger model) deferred; not this sprint.
- **Opus 4.6 cross-model not tested.** Cost-deferred. Plugin win now
  shown on 2 models; a 3rd (SOTA) model would further strengthen the
  generalization claim.
- **Gemma-4-31B untested.** Deferred.
- **ExampleSPE11b in E03 was a timeout (1200s), not a parse error.** The
  partial XML left on disk was unparseable because the agent was
  wall-clocked mid-write. Counts as 1 of E03's 5 timeouts. No
  additional investigation planned unless advisor asks.

### E20 hook-ablation (complete, XN-012)
- **Prior session's claimed hook was never functional.** Two bugs: (1)
  `hooks.json` had wrong schema (zod load error); (2) runner never passed
  `--plugin-dir`. Both fixed. Hook now routed via `--settings` (not
  `--plugin-dir`) to keep tool list identical across cells (RN-002 P1).
- **48-run ablation** (4 tasks × 3 independent runs × 4 cells
  {nohook / hook / noop / hook+noop}).
- **E17's 4/17 failure rate did NOT reproduce.** 0 `failed_no_outputs`
  in any cell's final status. Runner-retry salvaged the 2 first-attempt
  failures that did occur.
- **Mean TreeSim (fa0):** C0 0.643, C1 0.530, C2 0.595, C4 0.557. Hook
  trends **negative** (Δ −0.112 vs C0, p≈0.31 — n=12, underpowered).
- **Hook infrastructure validated** (fires on Stop, block schema works,
  event log populates); **headline "hook rescues empty completions" NOT
  supported.** Ship hook as defense-in-depth, do not paper-claim.
- **Decision (per D-004 branch 4):** do NOT expand to 17-task factorial.
  E17 was a single-seed fluke; no reproducible failure mode to rescue.
- **Dominant remaining failure mode = 900s timeout on
  `ExampleThermalLeakyWell`** (7/8 non-successes). Hook cannot rescue
  timeouts (fires on Stop, not docker kill).

*Last updated: 2026-04-21 (late evening — E20 complete; hook validated
as plumbing but not as rescue mechanism).*

### PAC-1 Phase A (2026-04-21 late — single-seed, v2+minimax, fa0 TreeSim on 17 test tasks)

Campaign framing (D-005, XN-013): establish that the {RAG, Memory, Self-Refinement} stack beats CC baseline AND each component contributes or doesn't hurt.

Seed-1 results:

| Cell | Config | Run | fa0 |
|:-:|---|---|---:|
| A1 | baseline | E16 noplug_mm_v2 | 0.497 |
| A2 | RAG only | E17 plug_mm_v2_seed2 | 0.440 |
| A3 | RAG+SR | **E23** pac1_plug_hook_s1 | **0.664** |
| A4 | RAG+Mem | E18 gmemsilent_mm_v2 | 0.725 |
| A5 | **FULL STACK** | **E24** pac1_plug_mem_hook_s1 | **0.317** |

**Key findings (single seed, caveats):**
- **Full stack (A5) loses to baseline (A1) by -0.180 paired on this seed.**
- Each component individually on top of RAG is positive: +SR alone +0.225 (A3-A2, diff seed), +Mem alone +0.286 (A4-A2, diff seed).
- **Combining Mem+SR with RAG is catastrophic: A5-A3 = -0.347 paired, 3/13 task W/L.** Same seed, only tool-list difference = memory tool present (uncalled).
- **Memory is never called** (mem=0 tool_use events in A4 AND A5). Not a retrieval-content effect.
- **A4-A5 confounded by TWO changes**: hook toggle + AskUserQuestion tool removed in intervening session.
- **Hook fires ~2× more in A5 than A3** (12 vs 7 hook events). Adding un-called memory tool correlates with more empty-completion attempts.

Multi-seed launched 20:22 to test whether the A5 regression reproduces.

*PAC-1 update 2026-04-21 late: stack fails headline on single seed; multi-seed pending.*

### PAC-1 FINAL (2026-04-21 end-of-sleep-session) — multi-seed complete

| Cell | Config | Seeds | Mean fa0 | Std | Δ vs A1 |
|:-:|---|:-:|---:|---:|---:|
| A1 | baseline (no-plug) | 1 | 0.497 | — | — |
| A2 | RAG only | 1 | 0.440 | — | -0.058 |
| **A3** | **RAG + SR** | 2 | **0.653** | **0.017** | **+0.155** |
| A4' | RAG + Mem (new infra) | 2 | 0.661 | 0.184 | +0.164 |
| **A5** | **FULL STACK** | 3 | **0.607** | 0.252 | **+0.110** |

**Headline findings (preliminary — n=1-3 per cell):**
1. **Full stack (A5) beats baseline on mean** by +0.110 fa0 TreeSim. Not yet statistically significant given σ=0.252 across 3 seeds.
2. **RAG+SR (A3) is the most reliable configuration**: +0.155 vs baseline at σ=0.017 across seeds. Essentially deterministic on this metric.
3. **RAG+Mem (A4') gains ~+0.16** at high variance (σ=0.184). Seed 1 = 0.791; seed 2 = 0.531 (hit by 2 parse errors).
4. **Components do not stack**: A5-A3 = -0.045; A5-A4' = -0.053. Adding the second component adds variance without net gain.
5. **SR's contribution is variance reduction**, not mean improvement beyond memory alone. Hook catches parse errors + empty completions that would otherwise tank fa0.
6. **RAG alone (A2) is ≤ baseline** under fa0. Plugin is only valuable when paired with SR or Mem.
7. **Memory tool is NEVER called** in any A4/A4'/A5 run. Its benefit is pure tool-list-shape, not retrieval.

**Revised paper framing (honest):**
- Claim A: "RAG + Self-Refinement robustly beats CC baseline (+0.16 fa0)" — *supported at low variance*.
- Claim B: "Silent memory via MCP tool helps via tool-list-shape" — *supported; mean +0.16 over plug; high variance*.
- Claim C (weakened): "Components do not stack additively — once RAG+SR (or RAG+Mem) is in place, adding the third yields ~zero marginal gain."
- Claim D: "SR is the variance-reduction component; Mem provides a marginal mean boost on good seeds."

**Open before any paper claim:** multi-seed A1, A2, A4' to n=3; adversarial review when codex installed; decompose fa0 variance by task.

*Last updated: 2026-04-21 end-of-sleep — PAC-1 Phase A + B1 complete (~$22, 8 new runs, n=1-3 per cell)*

### D-008 memory ablation sprint (2026-04-22 in progress)

User (via advisor's post-doc) flagged 3 issues with PAC-1 memory story:
vanilla CC's failure modes unexplained, memory impl was lexical ("hack
job"), G-Memory likely overkill. XN-014 characterizes 4 failure modes
(F1 schema hallucination, F2 wrong-version drift, F3 missing components,
F4 spec under-specification); RAG fixes F1, RAG alone introduces F2,
SR catches hard failures, nothing addresses F3/F4.

**D-007 → D-008 pivot after RN-003 adversarial review.** Original
design had 4 P1 blockers: 14/17 test tasks had leaked GT basenames in
memory_index.json; M0 "control" had null test exposure (memory tool
never called in A5); primer-size parity uncontrolled; n=2 A3 baseline
under-powered. D-008 addresses all: hygiene_audit.py gate + index
rebuild; M-placebo (equivalent-token generic text) added as control;
token parity preregistered at ≤10%; **A3 seed 3 launched and run:
0.267 — much lower than s1/s2 (0.664, 0.641).** A3 n=3 mean 0.524,
σ 0.221. Prior σ=0.017 was a 2-seed artifact. Paired-per-task
Wilcoxon replaces mean+std for the decision gate.

**Infrastructure:** `trajectory_grounder.py` produces per-trajectory
feedback (section scores, failure mode classification), distilled via
`google/gemini-3-flash-preview` (NOT minimax — breaks self-distillation
coupling). 4 artifacts produced + M-placebo + M3 embedding MCP, all
hygiene-audit-pass, token parity within 6.6% per pair. Vanilla-CC
trajectories on 18 training tasks harvested with extended blocklist
(all 55 test-GT basenames blocked) — 9 failures provide anti-pattern
content for grounded distillation.

**6 new conditions × 3 seeds = 18 runs** launched 2026-04-22T04:29Z,
running sequentially (~5-6h wall). Decision gate: best variant needs
≥ +0.05 mean vs A3 + Wilcoxon p ≤ 0.10. Claim B (attribution: grounding
contributes) requires (M1-g − M1-u) ≥ +0.04 OR (M4-g − M4-u) ≥ +0.04.

*Last updated: 2026-04-22 early morning — matrix running; awaiting scores.*

### D-008 results (2026-04-22 matrix complete)

**Headline: M1-u (monolithic DC-Cu primer) is the new hero harness addition.**

fa0 TreeSim (17 v2 tasks, minimax-m2.7, 3 seeds each unless noted):

| Condition | n | mean | std | Δ vs A3 | Wilcoxon p | wins | verdict |
|---|:-:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| A3 (RAG+SR) | 3 | 0.524 | 0.223 | — | — | — | baseline |
| M-placebo | 3 | 0.373 | 0.049 | −0.152 | 0.015 | 6/11 | content control passes |
| **M1-u (hero)** | **3** | **0.796** | **0.057** | **+0.272** | **<0.001** | **16/1** | **Claim A PASS** |
| M1-g | 3 | 0.766 | 0.046 | +0.242 | 0.003 | 13/4 | Claim A PASS |
| M4-u (valid) ¹ | 2 | 0.729 | 0.024 | +0.205 | TBD | TBD | Strong; structured items work at n=2 |
| M4-g | 3 | 0.469 | 0.299 | −0.055 | 0.145 | 5/12 | Real instability (see format finding) |
| M3-g ² | 1 valid | 0.281 | — | — | — | — | untestable |

¹ M4-u s3 excluded — OpenRouter 402 credit exhaustion mid-run.
² M3-g s2/s3 excluded — OpenRouter 403 weekly key limit.

**3 findings, 1 non-finding:**

1. **Content matters.** Placebo (generic GEOS text, token-matched to M1)
   HURTS (−0.152). M1's +0.272 lift is content-specific, not primer-shape.
2. **Primer format matters more than grounding.** Monolithic enumerated
   cheatsheet (M1, with solver-family → element-name table) beats
   structured RB-style items (M4-g, abstract principles without
   element-name enumeration). Per-task diagnostic: M4-g s2 invented
   `<CompressibleSolidCappedPlatesPorosity>` where M1-u correctly
   wrote `<CompressibleSolidParallelPlatesPermeability>`. Without the
   explicit vocabulary, the agent hallucinates element names — the F1
   failure class from XN-014.
3. **Memory-as-tool is dead on this task.** 0 `memory_lookup` calls in
   the valid M3-g seed (agent doesn't spontaneously reach for the
   memory MCP). Primer injection beats tool-locus in this model/harness.
4. **Grounding (TreeSim) did NOT produce attribution signal.** M1-g <
   M1-u and M4-g < M4-u on mean fa0. Claim B fails. Self-judged
   distillation by gemini-3-flash-preview is as effective as
   TreeSim-grounded distillation. The F3 missing-components sub-class
   is the only per-class delta where grounding helps (+0.302 vs +0.046).

**Paper framing shift:** ablation contribution is now the primer-format
axis (monolithic enumerated cheatsheet vs structured principle items),
not the grounding axis.

**Infra-failure cautionary tale:** 3 of 18 seeds contaminated by
OpenRouter 402/403. Fix: `scripts/memory/check_api_contamination.py`
scans any run dir, flags contaminated seeds. Run it before interpreting
any new campaign.

*Last updated: 2026-04-22 post-matrix — M1-u is hero; primer format is the real ablation; API contamination in 3 seeds flagged and excluded.*
